methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $4.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Saronic
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series D), post-money $9.25B

private.name: Epirus

data.narrative:
Chaos Industries, a Series D defense and security company founded by John Tenet and Bo Marr, is evaluated against a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Saronic, Flock Safety, Skydio, Epirus, and Hidden Level.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the company's $4.5B last post-money valuation, implying $6.8B. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $2.2B, with peer valuations ranging from $500M (Hidden Level, Series C) to $9.2B (Saronic, Series D). The peer cohort includes both Series D and Series E companies, introducing modest stage-related variance.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $1.9B to $7.8B, with a median implied value of $4.5B—coinciding with the current post-money valuation. The stage-progression method produces the highest estimate at $6.8B, while peer comparables suggest downside risk relative to current valuation. Key uncertainties include the composition and growth trajectories of the peer set, with Saronic at $9.2B representing a material outlier, and the limited availability of public company data in the sector. The current $4.5B valuation sits at the midpoint of the derived range, reflecting balanced risk.
